prompt: What did we hear about in emails this past week? Any commitments or issues?

observations: tools fired: intentPlanner, semanticSearch, sourceLookupIntentRouter, streamingToolPolicy, getRecentEmails
score.finalText:
I checked the mailbox for the past 7 days. Nothing came in.

What that means:
- **No received email threads** were found in the synced mailbox from **2026-05-05 through now**
- Source checked: **your mailbox email sync**
- Cutoff: data is current through **May 12, 12:18 PM CT**

So right now, there are **no email-based commitments or issues** I can verify from that mailbox for the last week.

Data gap:
- This appears to be the mailbox **test1@mail.com**
- If that’s not the mailbox you meant, the result is only telling us that **this specific synced mailbox** had no received mail

Next move:
- If you want, I can check a **specific person’s mailbox** if it’s synced, or I can search **email content by topic/project** instead of just “everything received this week,” which is usually more useful for finding actual commitments and problems.

data.message: Reading conversation memory and project context
Searching source records for the requested discussion context

output.summary: No emails received the last 7 days for test1@mail.com (mailbox).
output.dataCutoffNote: Data is current as of May 12, 12:18 PM CT (syncs run hourly). Emails received after that time won't appear here yet.

input.message: What did we hear about in emails this past week? Any commitments or issues?

output.rationale: The user is asking for what was heard in emails over a recent time window and specifically wants commitments or issues surfaced. This requires exact email-source retrieval and summarization of evidence. No project is selected, so a clarifying question is needed to determine scope (which project, team, or mailbox) before retrieving sources.

input.query: What did we hear about in emails this past week? Any commitments or issues?

output.query: What did we hear about in emails this past week? Any commitments or issues?

results.content:
[Email: Re: 440 site lighting]

Subject: Re: 440 site lighting
Date: 2026-04-28T12:09:32Z
From: Jesse Dawson <jdawson@alleatogroup.com>
To: Jason Welz <jason@tri-areaelectric.com>

Good Morning Jason, we are giving it one last go for pricing, they can either use us or we are in the same position and will move on.  They are going to be fined by the fire department for every day they don't have the life safety systems in place starting January 1, 2027 so I would think they have to release someone when this round of pricing is over.  We have other great projects  coming up that are negotiated work, the one in Savannah Georgia with Hotel and Collective will start design in 90 days, I think you said your crews work in that area. Thanks Jesse Dawson COO at Alleato Group  Mobile   502.612.2089   |   Email: jdawson@alleatogroup.com Web   www.alleatogroup.com   |         8383 Craig Street, Suite #150 Indianapolis, IN 46250  701 94 th  Ave. N, Suite #118 St. Petersburg, FL 33702     From: Jason Welz <jason@tri-areaelectric.com> Sent: Tuesday, April 28, 2026 7:45 AM To: Jesse Dawson <jdawson@alleatogroup.com> Subject: Fwd: 440 site lighting   Hi Jesse, Looks like we’re still running around on this…..Do you anticipate this being awarded anytime soon?  We’ve put quite a bit into this and while I want to keep prioritizing the turn-around requests, I also want to make sure we’re not spinning our wheels.  If you think this will be a Go, let me know and we’ll keep turning these around.  Thanks!  Jason

results.content:
[17:28] Jesse Dawson: Trying to run the jobs.
[17:31] Jesse Dawson: So there's that.
[17:32] Jesse Dawson: And then just a general comment, you know, we got a lot of, you know, a lot of things we do that we have our own freedoms on, but we always want to talk to our guys about, you know, if they're spending money, you know, I can't say as anything looks like it shouldn't be there, but, you know, just keeping them in mind, try and find a bargain once in a while if they can. [task]
[18:01] Jesse Dawson: I talked to Gregory, you know, about asking about these car rental charges and like, why is this so much? [question]
[18:08] Jesse Dawson: And he went and looked at it.
[18:09] Jesse Dawson: And he said they were actually charging.
[18:11] Jesse Dawson: Him more than his quoted rate.
[18:13] Jesse Dawson: So he's, you know, getting into them on that.
[18:16] Jesse Dawson: So things like that, you just want to talk to, you know, be cognizant of it.
[18:21] Jesse Dawson: I know the way we're set up right now, you don't even see the charges you guys make, which is until they hit your job budget. [task]
[18:31] Jesse Dawson: So we got to figure that out.
[18:34] Jesse Dawson: Because when I was a pm, I really, you know, if people were charging things, I was the one that decided what they were coded to just because I knew where there was money and where there wasn't.
[19:14] Jesse Dawson: Is it gpc? [question]
[19:16] Jesse Dawson: Is it Burnville? [question]
[19:18] Jesse Dawson: I'm guessing, but I don't have time.
[19:20] Jesse Dawson: Like, literally there's hundreds of transactions every month now.
[19:24] Jesse Dawson: I got to try and like, figure that out, you know, spending days trying to just get these receipts approved.
[19:32] Jesse Dawson: So he's got to find a better way.
[19:37] Kebba Mass: Yeah, I think we might do a weekly thing when we are doing. [task]
[19:41] Kebba Mass: Maybe on Fridays when we are going over the weekly reports, we might just go over quickly some of the those items. [task]
[19:50] Jesse Dawson: I'll ask in those, you guys, do you see everyone else's charges or just your own? [task, question]
[19:55] Jesse Dawson: You can see everyone's. [task]
[19:57] Jesse Dawson: Really? [question]
[19:58] Jesse Dawson: Share your screen, if you would.
